agent_notes: Lambeth clears PAINTS best for Museum & Gallery Days, Market Days, and Brixton/Vauxhall drinks; Books & Coffee is thin because many good bookshops lack on-site or literally adjacent independent coffee.

Creative Walks are deliberately limited: Brockwell Park is on the PAINTS Safety-First Shortlist, and the permitted South Bank to Tate Modern / Bankside riverside route starts in Lambeth but crosses into neighbouring Southwark.
Several venues publish hours and booking information but do not explicitly confirm 8-12 person walk-ins; these are marked UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.

venues.opening_hours: Official site says happy hour Wed-Sat 17:00-18:30 and active food bookings; exact full weekly hours UNVERIFIED - host must confirm before publishing.
venues.walk_in_policy: Official copy says bookings for food can be made, but 8-12 walk-ins are UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Likely feasible at early evening only; small Brixton Village room means host should call.
venues.capacity_notes: Compact Paris-inspired wine bar/cafe in Brixton Village; better as a hosted table or early weekday cluster.
venues.best_time_slot: Wednesday or Thursday 17:00-18:30 before dinner pressure.
venues.why_fits_paints: A small Brixton Village wine bar with natural-wine focus and enough Paris-cafe softness for strangers to settle in. It feels specific to the arcade rather than generic cocktail-led Brixton nightlife. The natural wine list gives Show & Tell an easy object to discuss.
venues.anecdote_story: Official site says the changing list champions natural wines from across Europe with a minimal-intervention focus.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Natural wines, martinis, small plates and cafe-style food.
venues.safety_notes: Brixton Village and Coldharbour Lane are high-footfall; use main-road routing to Brixton station and avoid late solo departures.
venues.backup_walk_time: Approx. 3 min by Google Maps walking search.
venues.risks_watchouts: Small venue and popular Brixton Village setting; group walk-in must be checked.

venues.opening_hours: Official site lists Mon 17:00-22:00, Tue 17:00-22:00, Wed 15:00-23:00, Thu 15:00-23:00; Fri-Sun continuation appears cut off in search extract, so full week UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.walk_in_policy: Official site frames it as 'everyone is a member'; group walk-ins for 8-12 are UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Likely suitable in the wine garden or early slot if called ahead.
venues.capacity_notes: Wine bar with garden signal; avoid peak Friday/Saturday nights.
venues.why_fits_paints: It has the right non-precious wine-club premise for a social table: wine without ceremony, cheese, charcuterie, and a Brixton side-street setting. It is close enough to backup options without feeling like a chain arcade stop. The tone is accessible, which matters for matched strangers.
venues.anecdote_story: Official slogan 'wine without the bullsh*t' gives the host a ready opener about making wine culture less gatekept.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Natural and classic wines, artisan cheese and charcuterie.
venues.safety_notes: Very near Brixton station and covered market footfall; keep to Vining Street/Atlantic Road after dark.
venues.backup_walk_time: Approx. 3 min by Google Maps walking search.
venues.risks_watchouts: Published hours extract incomplete; host must confirm current week and group acceptance.

venues.opening_hours: Official contact page lists bar Mon 18:00-23:00, Tue-Fri 11:00-23:00, Sat 11:00-23:00, Sun 12:00-22:00; kitchen windows listed separately.
venues.walk_in_policy: Official bookings page says some tables are held back for walk-ins; bookings up to 10 and larger groups by email, so 8-12 should call.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Good outside lunch/dinner peak with heads-up call; 12 may require email.
venues.capacity_notes: Proper pub/dining room; drinks-only tables allocated first come first served per SevenRooms extract.
venues.why_fits_paints: This is Lambeth's most robust character pub for Show & Tell: low-intervention wines, award-winning food, and a room with lived-in seriousness. It is local rather than scene-chasing. The table can talk about wine, pub food, Stockwell, and the independent pub group story.
venues.anecdote_story: Official site says Trish Hilferty and Charlie Bousfield opened it in 2010 after working at The Anchor and Hope, and that it has remained in the Top 50 Gastropub conversation.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Cask ales, cocktails, organic and low-intervention wines, seasonal pub food.
venues.safety_notes: South Lambeth Road is a lit main road between Stockwell and Vauxhall; direct arrivals to Stockwell or Vauxhall are recommended.
venues.backup_walk_time: Approx. 10 min by Google Maps walking search.
venues.risks_watchouts: Known pub can fill quickly; 8-12 needs a call even though walk-ins are held.

venues.opening_hours: Official site lists restaurant bar Tue-Sat 17:00-23:00, lunch Wed-Sat 12:00-14:30 and Sun 12:00-15:45, dinner Tue-Sat 18:00-21:45; Black Duke cellar bar open Tue-Thu from September 8 per site copy, with press saying Tue-Fri 18:00-23:00.
venues.walk_in_policy: Group bookings 8-24 are by reservation/email; Instagram/press mention walk-in only for cellar jazz/drinks, so PAINTS 8-12 walk-in is UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Suitable if PAINTS books or uses an early bar slot; fragile as pure walk-in.
venues.capacity_notes: Antique-filled Grade II* Georgian mansion with restaurant bar, terrace, cellar bar and private rooms.
venues.why_fits_paints: The room is the story: a Georgian mansion full of architectural salvage beside Vauxhall's traffic. It is visually exceptional and gives people instant objects to discuss. Operationally it is less walk-in-pure than Canton Arms, but the PAINTS fit is high.
venues.anecdote_story: Official site says the Grade II* Georgian mansion was built for the Duke of Brunswick in 1758.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Cocktails, wine, bar snacks, modern British restaurant food, architectural salvage interiors.
venues.safety_notes: Vauxhall transport is immediate and busy; use direct route to station after dark.
venues.backup_walk_time: Approx. 10 min by Google Maps walking search.
venues.risks_watchouts: Reservation/group policy is the limiting factor; cap as operationally fragile unless host confirms walk-in plan.

venues.opening_hours: Opening hours UNVERIFIED - host must confirm on official/social channels before publishing.
venues.walk_in_policy: Walk-in policy for 8-12 UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Likely better for a daytime/early pub table than evening peak.
venues.capacity_notes: Neighbourhood pub option for the Herne Hill/Brockwell edge; less wine-led than the Tier A picks.
venues.why_fits_paints: A neighbourhood pub gives the Herne Hill side of Lambeth a backup that is not over-designed. It is useful after Brockwell/Herne Hill activity. It is included as Tier B because the wine/story evidence is weaker.
venues.anecdote_story: No current official backstory found in the live search pass.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Pub drinks and food; exact current offer UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.safety_notes: Residential streets off Herne Hill/Loughborough Junction; use daylight or early evening and direct station routing.
venues.backup_walk_time: No qualifying same-category backup found within 10 min - closest stronger PAINTS alternative is Canton Arms at approx. 25 min by transit/walk.
venues.risks_watchouts: Included only as a geography counterweight; needs full host verification.

venues.opening_hours: Brixton Village page says the Village is open 7 days: Monday 08:00-18:00, Tuesday-Sunday 08:00-midnight; individual traders vary.
venues.walk_in_policy: Open-access covered market; meeting point walk-in should be confirmed.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Strong as a roaming market format; use a precise meeting point and small-group browsing.
venues.capacity_notes: Covered arcades with food, drink, retail, books and culture; trader opening varies.
venues.best_time_slot: Saturday 11:00-13:00 before lunch crush, or Thursday 18:00-20:00.
venues.why_fits_paints: It is obvious, but it is also Lambeth's most layered market: food, music, books, small units and arcade architecture. The covered lanes work in bad weather. PAINTS should curate it tightly rather than release the group into general wandering.
venues.anecdote_story: Brixton Market history traces the street market to the 1870s, with Brixton Village built as Granville Arcade in 1937.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Global food, independent shops, bars, music, events and market traders.
venues.safety_notes: High-footfall and busy; daylight or early evening only for first run, with Brixton station as fixed return point.
venues.backup_walk_time: Approx. 4 min by Google Maps walking search.
venues.risks_watchouts: Crowding and trader variability; avoid peak Saturday lunch unless hosted tightly.

venues.opening_hours: Listed by Lambeth as one of the borough markets; exact current weekly hours UNVERIFIED - host must confirm with operator before publishing.
venues.walk_in_policy: Open-access market.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Good for 8-12 as a short producer-led browse; weather exposure varies.
venues.capacity_notes: Street-market format on/near Brixton Station Road.
venues.best_time_slot: Sunday morning if operating; host must confirm date.
venues.why_fits_paints: A smaller producer market gives PAINTS a more local alternative to Brixton Village. It suits a slower tasting-and-talking format. The evidence base is thinner, so it needs host confirmation before launch.
venues.anecdote_story: Lambeth lists Brixton Farmers' Market among its active borough markets alongside Brixton Market and Lower Marsh.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Farm produce, food stalls and local market goods; trader mix UNVERIFIED.
venues.safety_notes: Busy central Brixton; use daytime and a fixed meet/end point.
venues.backup_walk_time: Approx. 4 min by Google Maps walking search.
venues.risks_watchouts: Operating hours and trader mix need direct operator confirmation.

venues.opening_hours: Official Southbank Centre page says open Friday-Sunday plus bank holiday Mondays; South Bank London lists spring/summer Fri 12:00-21:00, Sat 11:00-21:00, Sun/bank holidays 12:00-18:00 and winter Fri 12:00-20:00, Sat 11:00-20:00, Sun/bank holidays 12:00-18:00.
venues.walk_in_policy: Open-access market.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Good for 8-12 if the host controls crowd flow and seating expectations.
venues.capacity_notes: Around thirty food and drink stalls per South Bank London; seating is variable.
venues.why_fits_paints: A dependable market with strong cultural adjacency to Hayward, Royal Festival Hall and the river. It is not hidden, but it is operationally strong and weather-tolerant enough for a matched group. Best as a culture-plus-food route rather than only lunch.
venues.anecdote_story: Southbank Centre positions the market behind Royal Festival Hall, turning the arts complex into a weekend food commons.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Street food, bakery, drinks, global cuisines, coffee and juices.
venues.safety_notes: Very high-footfall, lit cultural estate; watch crowding and tourist congestion.
venues.backup_walk_time: Approx. 12 min by Google Maps walking search; within 15 min market backup allowance.
venues.risks_watchouts: Obvious and crowded in summer; not ideal for intimate discussion during peak lunch.

venues.opening_hours: Listed by Lambeth among borough markets; precise current trading days/hours UNVERIFIED - host must confirm with operator.
venues.walk_in_policy: Open-access street market.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Good as a casual lunch-market walk; limited shelter.
venues.capacity_notes: Street market near Waterloo with food stalls and independent shops.
venues.why_fits_paints: Lower Marsh is less polished than Southbank Centre, but it has a proper London street-market feel and strong transport. It works as a nearby backup for South Bank events. Tier B because current operator details need stronger confirmation.
venues.anecdote_story: Lambeth lists Lower Marsh among the borough's active markets, preserving the Waterloo street-market thread.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Street food and market stalls; current trader mix UNVERIFIED.
venues.safety_notes: Busy Waterloo streets; best daytime.
venues.backup_walk_time: Approx. 12 min by Google Maps walking search; within 15 min market backup allowance.
venues.risks_watchouts: Needs current operator/hours confirmation before use.

venues.opening_hours: Official site says Saturdays 10:00-15:00; local BID and Time Out have historically listed 10:00-16:00, so host should confirm current closing time.
venues.walk_in_policy: Open-access market.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Good for a producer-led browse; narrow street means keep the group moving.
venues.capacity_notes: Fresh produce, artisan food, street food, makers and flowers.
venues.why_fits_paints: A compact Saturday market with producer and maker emphasis, which fits the PAINTS 'market day' brief better than a general retail strip. It has enough nearby backups and an easy station. Clapham Common itself is not PAINTS-approved for walks, so keep the event on Venn Street.
venues.anecdote_story: This Is Clapham describes the market as working directly with farmers and small independent producers.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Produce, artisan food, street food, flowers and makers.
venues.safety_notes: Venn Street is pedestrianised and station-adjacent; avoid routing onto Clapham Common as a PAINTS walk.
venues.backup_walk_time: Approx. 12-15 min by Tube/bus; within market backup allowance by transit.
venues.risks_watchouts: Clapham Common is not on the Creative Walk safety list; do not convert this into a park event.

venues.opening_hours: Official site says first Sunday of every month, April-December, 10:00-16:00.
venues.walk_in_policy: Open-access volunteer-powered market festival.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Good for 8-12 as a multi-hub hosted route; needs clear start/end point.
venues.capacity_notes: Volunteer-powered street market festival with food, artisans, music and activities across hubs.
venues.why_fits_paints: It gives Lambeth a real community-market counterweight outside Brixton and South Bank. The volunteer-powered format and multiple hubs make it warmer than a shopping street. Seasonal schedule is the main constraint.
venues.anecdote_story: Lambeth and the official site describe Feast as a volunteer-powered, self-funded market festival celebrating local talent and the High Street.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Street food, arts, crafts, vintage, local produce, music and community activities.
venues.safety_notes: Daytime high-street event; use West Norwood station as the clear transit anchor.
venues.backup_walk_time: No same-category backup within 15 min walk; closest robust market alternative is Brixton Village at approx. 20 min by transit.
venues.risks_watchouts: Monthly and seasonal only; no tight same-category backup, so avoid using as the sole operational anchor.

venues.venue_name: Brockwell Park Walled Garden and Lido Loop
venues.opening_hours: Lambeth says Brockwell Park is normally open from 07:30 to 15 minutes before sunset; Friends of Brockwell Park note gates are no longer locked at night since November 2024, but PAINTS should use daytime only.
venues.walk_in_policy: Open-access park; PAINTS-approved Safety-First Shortlist park.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Good for 8-12 daytime walking with 3-4 stops and a fixed cafe fallback.
venues.capacity_notes: Suggested stops: Herne Hill gate, Brockwell Hall, Walled Garden / old kitchen garden, Art Deco Brockwell Lido.
venues.why_fits_paints: This is Lambeth's cleanest approved park walk: views, Victorian landscape, the walled garden, and the lido give the route visual rhythm. It is not just a green space; it has built stories at every stop. Use daylight because recent local discussion flags night-opening concerns.
venues.anecdote_story: Morus Londinium notes the Walled Garden was originally the kitchen garden of Brockwell Hall and probably dates from about 1812; recent press also notes Brockwell Lido's Art Deco and Grade II-listed status.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Park views, Brockwell Hall, Walled Garden, old mulberry story, Brockwell Lido exterior.
venues.safety_notes: On PAINTS Safety-First Shortlist. Daytime only; avoid after-dark routing because local reporting has raised concerns around night opening and unlit areas.
venues.backup_venue: Carnegie Cafe at Carnegie Library Hub
venues.backup_walk_time: Approx. 5 min from Herne Hill Road edge/start point by Google Maps walking search.
venues.risks_watchouts: Festival/event closures or park works can disrupt route; host must check Lambeth notices.

venues.venue_name: Queen's Walk: Lambeth Bridge to Blackfriars Bridge
venues.opening_hours: Open-access riverside public realm; South Bank London says Lambeth Bridge to Blackfriars Bridge is a relaxed 30-minute stroll.
venues.walk_in_policy: Open-access permitted non-park route under PAINTS riverside rule: South Bank to Tate Modern / Bankside.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Good daytime route with high footfall and many cultural fallback points.
venues.capacity_notes: Suggested stops: Lambeth Bridge view, Royal Festival Hall undercroft/Southbank Centre, Gabriel's Wharf/Oxo Tower, Blackfriars/Tate Modern approach.
venues.why_fits_paints: The route is culturally dense, safe-by-footfall, and easy to run with weather fallbacks. It gives a mix of river, modernist arts architecture and design-led wharf spaces. Tier B because it crosses out of Lambeth and is more obvious than Brockwell.
venues.anecdote_story: South Bank London notes the Queen's Walk from Lambeth Bridge to Blackfriars Bridge can be done in about 30 minutes; Coin Street describes Gabriel's Wharf as an arty riverside enclave with independent artists/design-led boutiques.
venues.serve_or_on_view: River views, Southbank Centre, skateboard undercroft, Gabriel's Wharf, Oxo Tower, Tate Modern approach.
venues.safety_notes: Permitted PAINTS non-park riverside route. High-footfall daytime route; avoid late-night isolated stretches under bridges.
venues.backup_walk_time: Approx. 5 min from Royal Festival Hall / mid-route start by Google Maps walking search.
venues.risks_watchouts: Part of route leaves Lambeth; host should frame it as Lambeth-start South Bank route.

venues.opening_hours: Southbank Centre venue page lists current/upcoming Hayward exhibitions; Anish Kapoor runs 16 Jun-18 Oct 2026. Date-specific timed-entry availability UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.walk_in_policy: Ticketed/timed gallery; walk-in availability for 8-12 is UNVERIFIED - host must confirm before event date.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Excellent if tickets are secured or walk-up availability confirmed; otherwise operationally fragile.
venues.capacity_notes: Large public gallery; group should arrive with tickets or timed-entry plan.
venues.best_time_slot: Friday 11:00-13:00 or relaxed-hours dates where appropriate.
venues.why_fits_paints: The exhibition itself justifies the event: immersive sculpture, voids, mirrors and monumental bodily forms are ideal prompts for conversation. Hayward's brutalist architecture adds another layer. It is obvious, but the current show is strong enough to absorb the penalty.
venues.anecdote_story: Lisson says Kapoor returns to the Hayward almost 30 years after his first major UK survey there, with new and seminal works across the whole gallery and terraces.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Anish Kapoor sculptures, paintings and immersive installations, 16 Jun-18 Oct 2026.
venues.safety_notes: Southbank Centre estate is high-footfall and transit-rich; crowd management is the main issue.
venues.backup_walk_time: Approx. 17 min walk; no qualifying museum/gallery backup within strict 10 min, so this remains operationally demoted if tickets are not secured.
venues.risks_watchouts: Ticketing and group entry must be confirmed; graphic/visceral works may not suit all audiences.

venues.venue_name: Garden Museum - Benton End: A Paradise of Pollen and Paint
venues.opening_hours: South Bank London lists open daily 10:00-17:00; exhibition runs 2 Jun-20 Sep 2026.
venues.walk_in_policy: Ticketed museum; walk-in / group 8-12 policy UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Strong if tickets/entry confirmed; cafe and garden support a 2-3 hour rhythm.
venues.capacity_notes: Museum, exhibition, garden and cafe in/around former church setting.
venues.why_fits_paints: Benton End is almost tailor-made for PAINTS: painting, plants, domestic community and a school of artists in one story. The museum's own garden gives conversation somewhere to land after the show. It is a high-fit cultural day with gentler energy than Hayward.
venues.anecdote_story: Art Fund says the exhibition recreates scenes from the postwar East Anglian School of Painting and Drawing at Benton End, run by Cedric Morris and Arthur Lett-Haines.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Benton End: A Paradise of Pollen and Paint, including Cedric Morris and the Benton End circle, 2 Jun-20 Sep 2026.
venues.safety_notes: Daytime Lambeth Palace Road / Westminster Bridge area is high-footfall; use main-road route to Lambeth North or Westminster.
venues.backup_walk_time: Approx. 2 min by Google Maps walking search.
venues.risks_watchouts: Ticketing and current entry conditions must be confirmed; obvious press attention adds penalty.

venues.venue_name: Newport Street Gallery - Jack White: THESE THOUGHTS MAY DISAPPEAR
venues.opening_hours: Official site lists upcoming exhibition Jack White: THESE THOUGHTS MAY DISAPPEAR, 29 May-13 Sep 2026; exact opening hours for the date UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.walk_in_policy: Admission is free per official site; group walk-in policy UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Likely suitable as a free gallery visit if exhibition is open; check group flow.
venues.capacity_notes: Large renovated Victorian scenery-painting studio galleries.
venues.why_fits_paints: The building has a strong physical story and the show gives a current focal point. It is free, visually clean and close to Vauxhall backup galleries. The Damien Hirst collection association is a conversation hook without needing to over-explain.
venues.anecdote_story: Official site says the gallery presents works from Damien Hirst's collection in a renovated Victorian scenery-painting studio.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Jack White: THESE THOUGHTS MAY DISAPPEAR, 29 May-13 Sep 2026.
venues.safety_notes: Short walk from Vauxhall; use daytime or early evening and direct route via main streets.
venues.backup_walk_time: Approx. 8 min by Google Maps walking search.
venues.risks_watchouts: Current show press is thinner than Hayward/Garden Museum; host should verify opening days.

venues.opening_hours: Official and Art Fund pages list Wed-Sun 12:00-18:00 during exhibitions or by appointment; New Exhibitions lists Gabriel Abrantes: Bardo Loops, 16 Apr-2 Aug 2026.
venues.walk_in_policy: Free admission; group 8-12 walk-in UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Good for a compact contemporary-art visit, but gallery capacity may be limited.
venues.capacity_notes: Small contemporary gallery/residency space; avoid crowding by calling.
venues.why_fits_paints: Gasworks is less obvious than the South Bank institutions and has a real artist-residency backbone. Its shows can be knotty in a productive way for discussion. It also pairs well with Newport Street and Vauxhall drinks.
venues.anecdote_story: New Exhibitions describes Gasworks as a Vauxhall contemporary art space; the official site anchors it at 155 Vauxhall Street with free admission during exhibitions.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Gabriel Abrantes: Bardo Loops, 16 Apr-2 Aug 2026.
venues.safety_notes: Daytime route between Oval/Vauxhall; use main roads for after-gallery transfer.
venues.backup_walk_time: Approx. 8 min by Google Maps walking search.
venues.risks_watchouts: Small space; verify that exhibition is open and suitable for group dwell time.

venues.venue_name: Black Cultural Archives - The Forgotten Generations Exhibition
venues.opening_hours: Official events page lists The Forgotten Generations Exhibition dates in Jun-Jul 2026; Visit London lists normal opening Tue-Sat 10:00-18:00, but official contact/about pages say visits are by appointment only, so host must confirm.
venues.walk_in_policy: Appointment/ticketing conflict: official pages say appointment only; Tier B and host must book/confirm group access.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Good if booked; not suitable as spontaneous walk-in.
venues.capacity_notes: Archive/gallery at Windrush Square; current exhibition has set event dates.
venues.why_fits_paints: The story depth is exceptional, and Windrush Square gives the venue a civic setting. The Forgotten Generations subject offers a meaningful conversation frame. It is Tier B only because the access model conflicts with PAINTS walk-in robustness.
venues.anecdote_story: Official events page says The Forgotten Generations was created by local Black RAF veteran Donald Campbell to honour under-recorded experiences of Black people living in the UK.
venues.serve_or_on_view: The Forgotten Generations Exhibition, selected dates Jun-Jul 2026.
venues.safety_notes: Windrush Square is central and high-footfall; use daytime or early evening.
venues.backup_walk_time: Approx. 4 min by Google Maps walking search.
venues.risks_watchouts: Appointment-only language means PAINTS cannot treat this as walk-in without confirmation.

venues.opening_hours: Official gallery page lists future Select/26, 3-26 Sep 2026; Instagram profile lists Tue-Sat 10:00-18:00. Current July exhibition availability UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.walk_in_policy: Public gallery access appears available during exhibitions, but 8-12 walk-in UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Best for a compact photo exhibition or as backup to Black Cultural Archives.
venues.capacity_notes: Photo co-operative/gallery with courses, darkrooms and exhibitions.
venues.why_fits_paints: Photofusion gives Brixton a specialist image-making venue rather than another food/drink stop. The co-operative history and photography focus suit visual conversation. Tier B because current July show evidence is not strong enough.
venues.anecdote_story: British Journal of Photography described Photofusion as a legacy photo co-operative in Brixton, set up in 1990 and committed to image-makers.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Photography exhibitions, courses, darkrooms and image-making culture; July 2026 show UNVERIFIED.
venues.safety_notes: Central Brixton; use daytime and direct station routing.
venues.backup_walk_time: Approx. 4 min by Google Maps walking search.
venues.risks_watchouts: Do not schedule until a current exhibition is confirmed.

venues.opening_hours: Official about page lists Sun-Fri 11:00-17:30, Sat 09:30-17:30; Brixton Village estate is open broader hours but individual store applies.
venues.walk_in_policy: Bookshop walk-ins accepted in normal retail model; 8-12 seated cafe/bar use is UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Good for browsing and short discussion; cafe/bar seating and two-hour stay must be confirmed.
venues.capacity_notes: Inclusive children's/YA/community bookshop in Brixton Village; crowdfunder says cafe, bar, events and workshops are part of expansion aim.
venues.why_fits_paints: Round Table is the strongest Lambeth Books & Coffee fit because the literary identity is specific: inclusive books and community, not just shelves as decor. Brixton Village supplies coffee/food backup around it. It has real mission and event energy.
venues.anecdote_story: Storehouse Voices notes Round Table Books opened in 2019 and focuses on stories featuring Black people and other people of colour.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Inclusive books, events/community programming; cafe/bar status to be confirmed.
venues.safety_notes: Covered Brixton Village; use station-adjacent route.
venues.backup_walk_time: Approx. 3 min by Google Maps walking search.
venues.risks_watchouts: On-site seating/coffee details are not fully confirmed; host must call.

venues.opening_hours: Bookmongers official site confirms address and online book ordering but not current shop hours in extract; F Mondays lists 436 Coldharbour Lane Mon-Fri 08:00-16:00, Sat-Sun 09:00-16:00. Host must confirm Bookmongers hours.
venues.walk_in_policy: Bookshop and coffee shop are walk-in retail/cafe formats; 8-12 seating for two hours at F Mondays is UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Good as browse-then-coffee in two neighbouring units; not guaranteed as one seated table.
venues.capacity_notes: Second-hand bookshop plus independent coffee a few doors away.
venues.why_fits_paints: Bookmongers has the paper-dense, slightly chaotic charm PAINTS wants, and F Mondays supplies a credible independent coffee partner almost next door. The pairing is more atmospheric than operationally seamless. It works best as a browse-and-regroup format.
venues.anecdote_story: The Indiependent says Bookmongers has stocked South London shelves since 1992; Time Out describes Patrick Kelly as the American-born founder behind the shop's 30-year following.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Second-hand books; independent coffee from F Mondays on Coldharbour Lane.
venues.safety_notes: Central Brixton high-footfall; use daytime/early afternoon.
venues.backup_walk_time: Approx. 3 min by Google Maps walking search.
venues.risks_watchouts: Bookshop hours and coffee seating need confirmation; popular/obvious shop adds penalty.

venues.venue_name: Carnegie Cafe at Carnegie Library Hub
venues.opening_hours: Carnegie Cafe page lists Mon-Fri 08:30-17:00, Sat 09:00-17:00, Sun 10:00-15:00; Lambeth lists Carnegie Library Mon 14:00-19:00, Tue/Thu 10:00-13:00 and 14:00-19:00, Wed/Fri 10:00-13:00 and 14:00-18:00, Sat 09:00-13:00 and 14:00-17:00, Sun closed.
venues.walk_in_policy: Cafe/lounge says anyone can relax and it is not necessary to buy to sit; group 8-12 and reserved areas require host confirmation.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Likely suitable for daytime coffee-and-books if library/cafe hours overlap.
venues.capacity_notes: Community cafe/lounge inside a Grade II listed public library; areas can be reserved for community groups.
venues.why_fits_paints: This is not a bookshop, but it is a real books-and-coffee environment with public-library shelves, cafe, garden and community history. It is a useful Lambeth south/east counterweight to Brixton. Tier B because retail bookshop energy is absent.
venues.anecdote_story: Lambeth calls Carnegie a picturesque Grade II listed library on Herne Hill Road; local history records it opened in 1906 and had a community campaign around closure/reopening.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Library books, cafe coffee/tea/cakes/lunch, community lounge and garden.
venues.safety_notes: Residential Herne Hill Road; use daytime and station/bus routing.
venues.backup_walk_time: No qualifying books-and-coffee backup within 10 min; closest stronger PAINTS alternative is Bookmongers + F Mondays at approx. 20 min by bus/walk.
venues.risks_watchouts: Library and cafe hours do not fully overlap; host must verify both.

venues.venue_name: Clapham Books + Venn Street Coffee Pairing
venues.opening_hours: Official site lists Mon-Fri 10:00-18:00, Sat 10:00-18:00, Sun 10:00-16:00.
venues.walk_in_policy: Bookshop retail walk-ins likely; literal next-door independent coffee/seating for 8-12 is UNVERIFIED - host must confirm, so Tier B.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Suitable for browse-first only; coffee partner must be confirmed.
venues.capacity_notes: Independent bookshop near Clapham Common/Venn Street, with nearby independent cafes but no confirmed on-site coffee.
venues.best_time_slot: Saturday 10:00-12:00 paired with Venn Street Market.
venues.why_fits_paints: The bookshop is credible and gives Clapham a literary anchor, but the coffee rule is the weak link. It can support a hybrid with Venn Street Market rather than a pure two-hour cafe stay. Tier B only.
venues.anecdote_story: Official site presents Clapham Books as an independent bookshop on Clapham Common with a Herne Hill sister shop.
venues.serve_or_on_view: New books and independent bookshop browsing; coffee partner UNVERIFIED.
venues.safety_notes: Station-adjacent and busy; do not use Clapham Common itself as a PAINTS walk.
venues.backup_walk_time: No qualifying books-and-coffee backup within 10 min; closest stronger option is Round Table Books by Tube/bus.
venues.risks_watchouts: Fails strict on-site/next-door coffee until a specific partner is confirmed.

rejected_candidates.reason_rejected: Explicitly listed in PAINTS v6 as removed/do-not-source for Creative Walks.
Not on PAINTS-approved Premium or Safety-First park list, so cannot be sourced as Creative Walk.
Strong history but not on PAINTS-approved park list; do not source or use as backup for Creative Walks.
Not on PAINTS-approved park list; cannot be included despite local appeal.
Too tourist-led and hotel/attraction-heavy for PAINTS; weaker paintings/sculpture/craft exhibition fit than Hayward, Garden Museum or Newport Street.

rejected_candidates.venue_name: Black Cultural Archives as walk-in primary
rejected_candidates.reason_rejected: Not rejected outright, but rejected as Tier A primary because official pages state appointment-only visits while Visit London lists broader opening hours; included only as Tier B with booking caveat.
Chain bookshop and no verified independent on-site/next-door coffee fit; fails PAINTS independent/character filter.
Interesting cultural archive, but tour/archive model is bookable rather than walk-in exhibition-led; weaker current paintings/sculpture/craft fit.
Artist-run gallery is PAINTS-coded, but current official page showed offsite exhibition in Finland rather than a robust Lambeth visit; held as future review rather than included.
